mnemonics.title: Backpropagation
mnemonics.content: Backpropagation is like climbing a mountain. You start at the bottom (input layer), climb up (forward pass), reach the top (output layer), and then slide back down (backward pass) to adjust your path (update weights).

acronyms.usage: BP is an algorithm used to train artificial neural networks.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a ball rolling down a hill. The ball represents the error, and the hill represents the loss function. As the ball rolls down, it gains speed (gradient) and eventually reaches the bottom (optimal solution).

analogies.analogy: Backpropagation is like a game of telephone. You start with a message (input), pass it through multiple people (layers), and eventually get a distorted message (output). Backpropagation helps adjust the message to match the original.
